g.data-narration: Welcome to Inter-Cloud Resource Management. We'll explore how organizations leverage multiple cloud providers.
Inter-cloud computing distributes services across multiple cloud environments for seamless resource sharing.
g.text: A model where cloud services are distributed across multiple cloud environments
enabling seamless sharing of resources, data, and applications between
different platforms (public, private, or hybrid clouds).

g.data-narration: We have three core concepts: Cloud Federation for resource sharing, Cloud Bursting for peak demand, and Resource Provisioning.
The Broker-Based Architecture uses an intermediary between consumers and providers for service discovery and resource management.
g.text: Cloud broker acts as intermediary: service discovery → SLA negotiation →
resource allocation → monitoring across multiple cloud providers.

g.data-narration: In Peer-to-Peer Architecture, clouds directly exchange resources without a central broker, requiring standardized protocols.
g.text: Cloud providers directly interact to share resources without a central broker.
Requires standardized protocols for communication and resource exchange.

g.data-narration: Hybrid Architecture combines broker-based and peer-to-peer models for flexible cloud interactions and resource sharing.
g.text: Combines elements of both broker-based and peer-to-peer models,
offering flexibility in how clouds interact and share resources.

g.data-narration: Four essential components: Resource Discovery finds available resources, Selection allocates optimally, Monitoring tracks usage, Security manages identity.
Three key protocols enable inter-cloud communication: OCCI for resource management, CDMI for data, CIMI for infrastructure.
Four scheduling strategies: Cost-Aware minimizes expenses, Performance-Oriented maximizes speed, Energy-Efficient is green, Hybrid combines all.
Five major challenges face inter-cloud: interoperability issues, security concerns, performance variability, vendor lock-in, and complex management.
Real-world implementations include Kubernetes Federation, Apache Mesos, Cloud Management Platforms, and Cloud Brokerage Services.
Case Study: E-commerce company uses cloud bursting during holiday sales - monitoring triggers automatic public cloud provisioning.
g.text: Case Study: Cloud Bursting for E-Commerce
E-commerce company with 10x traffic during holidays uses private cloud plus burst to public.

g.data-narration: Future trends include serverless cross-cloud functions, AI-driven optimization, blockchain for resource exchange, and edge cloud integration.
To summarize: Inter-cloud enables resource sharing across providers through broker, P2P, or hybrid architectures using OCCI, CDMI, CIMI protocols.
g.text: ✓ Inter-cloud computing distributes services across multiple cloud environments
✓ Three architectures: Broker-Based, Peer-to-Peer, and Hybrid models
✓ Four key components: Discovery, Selection, Monitoring, Security
✓ Standard protocols: OCCI, CDMI, CIMI enable interoperability
✓ Scheduling strategies: Cost-aware, Performance, Energy-efficient, Hybrid

g.data-narration: Exam tips: Understand cloud bursting versus federation, focus on interoperability and security, know architectural trade-offs, remember protocols OCCI CDMI CIMI.
g.text: • Distinguish cloud bursting (temporary) versus federation (formal) versus multi-cloud strategies
• Focus on challenges: interoperability, security, privacy, performance variability - frequently tested

g.data-narration: Comparison: Broker-based offers centralized management but single point of failure. P2P is resilient but complex. Hybrid offers flexibility with maximum complexity.
